name: iFlytek Spark V4.0 (Xinghuo)
archDisplay: Proprietary transformer, trained fully on domestic (Huawei Ascend) compute
note: GPT-4-Turbo-class flagship trained on fully domestic compute; later refreshed as Spark 4.0 Turbo in October 2024.
verificationNotes:
Confirmed via TechNode, Caixin Global, ChinaDaily, and IoT Now: launched June 27, 2024 in Beijing, positioned as rivaling/surpassing GPT-4 Turbo on several benchmarks, trained exclusively on the Huawei-cobuilt domestic compute platform (no Nvidia dependency) due to US chip export restrictions. Spark 4.0 Turbo follow-up in Oct 2024 also confirmed. No results rows were present to verify. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): Conducted a final, methodologically-diversified search for iFlytek Spark V4.0 (Xinghuo) benchmark data, per the task instructions, and found NO citable numeric result to add. Read the existing corpus file first (corpus/models/iflytek/iflytek-spark-v4-0-xinghuo.json) — confirmed it already has zero results and prior verificationNotes document earlier English-only searches.

This round widened to Chinese-language sources as instructed:
- Chinese tech press: 腾讯新闻 (news.qq.com), 央广网 (cnr.cn), 21世纪经济报道, OFweek, 澎湃新闻, CSDN, 果壳 (guokr.com), 知乎 (zhihu — blocked by 403), 中国财富网, 浙江在线, 界面新闻 (jiemian.com)
- Chinese leaderboards: SuperCLUE (superclueai.com, cluebenchmarks.com, 2024 H1 report via aigcrank.cn) — Spark V4.0 does NOT appear in the SuperCLUE 2024 H1 (June 2024) results table at all; a July 2024 SuperCLUE report states Qwen2-72B-Instruct surpassed "Wenxin 4.0, iFlytek Spark V4.0" without giving Spark's own score.
- C-Eval leaderboard (cevalbenchmark.com) — explicitly notes iFlytek's Xinghuo model was "not authorized to be evaluated and publicized" on C-Eval.
- OpenCompass/司南 (opencompass.org.cn, rank.opencompass.org.cn) — only found "CompassArena" weekly community-voting rankings (Spark placed top-3 several weeks, 2nd twice) — this is a qualitative voting leaderboard, not a numeric benchmark score, and isn't the LMArena/Chatbot Arena the taxonomy's "arena" slug refers to.
- Official iFlytek press materials: businesswire.com launch release, edu.iflytek.com transcript of Liu Qingfeng's keynote, cnr.cn official release coverage — these disclose only internal/proprietary comparative deltas (e.g. "Chinese text understanding improved from 598→702 vs Spark V3.5") with no named public benchmark, and marketing claims like "ranked #1 in 8 of 12 domestic/international mainstream test sets, surpassing GPT-4 Turbo" without ever naming which 8 test sets or giving scores.

Also checked per the task's general steps:
- Artificial Analysis — no Spark V4.0 entry found in search results.
- LMArena/Chatbot Arena — Spark not present among currently listed/ranked models found via search.
- OpenRouter — no evidence iFlytek Spark models are listed on OpenRouter at all.
- Wayback Machine — web.archive.org is not fetchable from this environment (tool explicitly blocks the domain), so could not check archived snapshots of iFlytek's launch page.

One tangential lead: Stanford HAI's 2025 AI Index reportedly says Spark 4.0 was "the only Chinese model in MixEval-Hard's top 10," but (a) MixEval-Hard is not in the allowed taxonomy slug list, and (b) I could not find a numeric score for it, only the qualitative claim.

All Chinese-press articles about the June 27, 2024 V4.0 launch (and the later Oct 2024 V4.0 Turbo refresh) repeat the same unsourced "8/12" or "9/14 international test sets, #1" marketing claim without ever naming the benchmarks or publishing scores — this pattern is consistent enough across a dozen independent outlets that it appears to be iFlytek's standard press messaging rather than omitted detail. No independent third-party numeric benchmark result (matching any taxonomy slug) was found anywhere.

Conclusion: this appears to be a genuine case of a vendor that never published standard, independently-verifiable benchmark scores for this specific model — consistent with the "valid, expected outcome" the task anticipated. Recommend the corpus entry remain at zero tracked results unless iFlytek publishes an actual scores table in the future. ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): Checked the two specific structured sources requested for iFlytek Spark V4.0 (Xinghuo) — no results found in either:

1. Epoch AI raw data feed: fetched both https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json (179KB, benchmark-definition metadata only, no model-score rows) and https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv (2.29MB, the actual per-model-run results file with columns like Model/Organization/mean_score/best_score). Grepped/parsed both files case-insensitively for "iflytek", "spark", and "xinghuo" in any form. Zero matches for iFlytek or Xinghuo. The only "spark" hits in the CSV are "Muse Spark" (Meta AI, unrelated) and an unrelated paper titled "Spark: Scaling Towards Personal Superintelligence." Confirmed via direct Python parse of the Organization/Model columns that no iFlytek entries exist anywhere in the dataset.

2. Vals.ai: fetched https://www.vals.ai/models — the models list shows OpenAI, ... [truncated; see round13_result.json for full text]

[ROUND 20 — HF-raw-content + academic-citation search, 2026-07-16] Read corpus/models/iflytek/iflytek-spark-v4-0-xinghuo.json first — confirmed zero results and reviewed 20+ prior verificationNotes rounds (general web, Chinese-language search, SuperCLUE/C-Eval/OpenCompass, Epoch AI raw CSV/JSON, Vals.ai). This round ran exactly the two requested source types and found no addable taxonomy-benchmark result.

1) HuggingFace model card check — NO HF repo exists for this model.
- Fetched https://huggingface.co/iFLYTEK (the vendor's official org page): page explicitly states "None public yet" under Models — zero public repos of any kind.
- Ran HF search for "xinghuo" (huggingface.co/models?search=xinghuo) — no relevant results.
- Ran HF search for "spark v4" (huggingface.co/models?search=spark+v4) — returned only unrelated repos (DeepSeek-V4-Flash-Spark-GGUF forks, an unrelated "Spark-5M-Base-v4" toy model, Spark-TTS variants) — none are iFlytek's Xinghuo/Spark V4.0.
- This is expected: Spark V4.0 is a closed, proprietary, API-only model (per the existing corpus entry: openness="closed", apiAvailable=true, no weights ever released), so no HF model card / eval_results.json exists to check "Files and versions" on. Confirmed absence rather than assumed it.

2) Academic citation search (Semantic Scholar + Google Scholar) — found ONE real citing paper with a fetched table, but it does not map to any taxonomy benchmark slug.
- Semantic Scholar Graph API (api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1/paper/search) returned HTTP 429 "Too Many Requests" on every attempt across ~8 tries (including a 6-attempt exponential-backoff retry loop up to 30s between attempts) — the public API appears to be globally rate-limited in this environment; never got a successful response, so could not use it directly.
- Fell back to Google Scholar (scholar.google.com/scholar) per the task's own fallback instruction. Searches for "iFlytek Spark V4.0" and "iFlytek Spark 4.0" benchmark surfaced several 2024-2026 papers citing the model.
- Fetched the most promising hit directly: AutoIoT: Automated IoT Platform Using Large Language Models (Cheng, Xu, Zhang, Li, Wang, Yang; arXiv:2411.10665, Nov 2024). Read the full 12-page PDF. Confirmed reference [47] = "iFLYTEK. Spark. [Online]. Available: https://xinghuo.xfyun.cn/", and the paper explicitly names the model "iFLYTEK Spark 4.0" in its methods and Table VI. Quoted exact table (p.10, "TABLE VI: THE TIME COST RATIO OF INFORMATION EXTRACTION, RULE GENERATION, AND CODE GENERATION WITH DIFFERENT LLMS"):
  Qwen 2.5: 25.06 / 31.14 / 43.80
  iFLYTEK Spark 4.0: 17.06 / 21.67 / 61.27
  Dou Bao: 13.76 / 18.36 / 67.88
  ChatGPT-4o: 14.90 / 32.60 / 52.50
  These are "time cost ratios" (relative proportions of wall-clock latency for three custom IoT-automation subtasks — device info extraction, rule generation, code generation), explicitly NOT an accuracy/score metric — the paper states raw latency comparisons were invalid due to network variance so only relative ratios were reported. This is a real, citable, fetched number for Spark V4.0, but it doesn't correspond to any benchmark in the allowed taxonomy (not bigcodebench/livecodebench/humaneval-style accuracy, not any agentic/tool-use benchmark on the list) — it's a bespoke efficiency metric from one paper's own pipeline, not a named public benchmark. Did not add it.
- Other Google Scholar hits reviewed by title/snippet only (not deep-fetched, since abstracts gave no indication of numeric Spark V4.0 scores on a named benchmark): "A benchmark for evaluating crisis information generation capabilities in LLMs" (Han et al., 2025/2026, since retitled "Which LLM performs best in crisis knowledge generation?" in ScienceDirect) — uses a custom CKEval framework, not a taxonomy benchmark. "Research on the training and application methods of a lightweight agricultural domain-specific LLM" (Pan/Zhang/Chen, Appl. Sci. 2024, 14(13):5764) — published July 2024, essentially simultaneous with V4.0's June 27, 2024 launch, so likely references an earlier Spark version if any; could not fetch full text (mdpi.com returned 403, doi.org redirect to mdpi also blocked) to confirm which Spark version or benchmark, so not counted. Two other hits ("Unpacking AI Chatbot Dependency," "Understanding perceived intelligence...healthcare chatbots") are HCI/psychology papers unlikely to report benchmark scores and were not pursued further given time constraints.
- Also checked EduEval (arXiv:2512.00290, Nov 2025) — a Chinese K-12 education benchmark paper that evaluates 14 LLMs — but it evaluates "Spark-X1" (iFlytek's separate reasoning model, corpus/models/iflytek/iflytek-spark-x1-xinghuo-x1.json), not "Spark V4.0", so its results (Spark-X1 ranked #1 overall, avg 81.1%) belong to a different corpus entry, not this one.

Conclusion: Genuinely exhausted both requested source types. iFlytek Spark V4.0 has no HuggingFace presence at all (confirmed via the org's own "None public yet" page). It does appear as a baseline in at least one indexed academic paper (AutoIoT, arXiv:2411.10665) with real fetched numbers, but those numbers are a custom latency-ratio metric with no taxonomy-slug mapping, so no corpus addition is warranted from it. Recommend the entry remain at zero tracked results; if a future round wants to chase the agricultural-domain paper (Appl. Sci. 2024, 14(13):5764) it would need a way past MDPI's 403/anti-bot block to check whether it references V4.0 specifically and on what metric.

[ROUND 21 — light delta-check, 2026-07-16] Prior round (ROUND 20) was dated the same day, so this was a light re-check only, not a full re-derivation, per task instructions. Ran two targeted web searches ("iFlytek Spark V4.0 Xinghuo benchmark score 2026" and "iFlytek Spark 4.0 MMLU GPQA benchmark leaderboard") plus a Chinese-language search for third-party test writeups ("讯飞星火V4.0" 实测 跑分 评测). All hits are the same June/July 2024 launch-era coverage already catalogued in prior rounds (TechNode, 腾讯新闻, OFweek, 新浪, laitimes) repeating the unsourced "ranked #1 in 8 of 12 international test sets, surpasses GPT-4 Turbo" marketing claim, plus a laitimes/OFweek/腾讯新闻 "实测" (hands-on) article about V4.0's "个人空间" (Personal Space) feature that discusses qualitative UX, not numeric benchmark scores. No new numeric taxonomy-benchmark result (mmlu/gpqa/hle/math/aime/humaneval/livecodebench/swe-bench/mmmu) surfaced. Reaffirms the ROUND-20 conclusion: no addable citable score exists for this model; entry remains at zero tracked results.
